reasoning: As of the June 5, 2025 knowledge cutoff, Casey DeSantis has not announced a run for governor. Although Ron DeSantis teased in April that she would announce her plans after the early-May end of the legislative session, that timeline has passed. Instead, on May 20, she explicitly stated she is in "no hurry" to make a decision and has "plenty of time," noting that people are "tired of politics." Furthermore, mid-May reporting from the Miami Herald indicates she is "leaning against" running as "doubts mount." This hesitation comes after Rep. Byron Donalds officially entered the race, received Donald Trump's endorsement, and subsequently took a 19-point lead over her in polling (44% to 25%). Finally, the 'Hope Florida' initiative she champions is currently facing scrutiny and a criminal investigation, dealing another blow to her near-term viability. With only 25 days remaining until the July 1, 2025 deadline, her explicit desire to delay the decision makes an announcement in the next few weeks highly improbable.
